Event: Starts up a pet adoption to avoid filling in for Leslie at government meetings
Explanation: She dodges boring meetings yet creates a successful adoption effort that helps animals and citizens.

Event: Imitates Leslie’s presentation style to cope with public spotlight
Explanation: Using Leslie’s persona helps her manage nerves and function publicly, with no clear harm to others.

Event: Stops imitating Leslie and starts acting like herself in presentations
Explanation: She’s more effective and comfortable, despite complaints about rudeness, so net gain for her and her work.

Event: Constantly insults, makes fun of, and belittles Ann
Explanation: She gains amusement and ego boost while repeatedly hurting Ann socially/emotionally.

Event: Turns to Ann for advice despite treating her badly
Explanation: She benefits from Ann’s guidance and the dynamic slowly becomes a mutual, semi-jokey friendship.

Event: Admits she loves Ann but mumbles and begs her not to repeat it
Explanation: Honesty deepens their bond and costs her only mild embarrassment.

Event: Eventually has a son Jack and another child with Andy
Explanation: The story presents the children as part of a positive “babies ever after” outcome for them.

Event: Shows disdain for people but actively helps cats and dogs get adopted
Explanation: Her misanthropy doesn’t stop her from building a high-impact, fulfilling animal-help project.

Event: Encourages Andy to pursue his dreams
Explanation: Her support advances Andy’s growth and career, and she benefits from a more capable partner.

Event: Insults and embarrasses other people (especially Jerry) for fun
Explanation: She entertains herself at the repeated expense of coworkers’ dignity and comfort.

Event: Uses hints, subterfuge, and jealousy ploys instead of telling Andy she likes him
Explanation: Her avoidance of direct communication prolongs problems and confusion in the relationship.

Event: Asks “why does everything we do have to be cloaked in 15 layers of irony?” after gay boyfriends act like jerks
Explanation: Recognizing and challenging her own over-ironic lifestyle helps her move toward more authentic connections.

Event: Attempts earnestly to forge a career as she realizes she can’t be a jerk to everyone
Explanation: Choosing ambition and professionalism benefits her trajectory and improves her relationships at work.

Event: Encourages Andy’s independence by not being a caregiver in the way Ann was
Explanation: Her different style pushes Andy to mature and become more self-sufficient, improving their relationship.

Event: Rejects her two gay boyfriends for being artificial and mean, and chooses Andy instead
Explanation: Leaving a toxic, performative relationship for a kind partner is good for her and for Andy.

Event: Breaks up with her “gay boyfriend” Derek when she realizes he and Ben are mean
Explanation: She removes herself from a negative dynamic and stops reinforcing their nastiness.

Event: Encourages Andy to believe growing up can still fit who they are
Explanation: They reconcile responsibility with authenticity, improving their future prospects and relationship stability.

Event: Adopts three-legged dog Champion with Andy
Explanation: The adoption increases their happiness and gives the disabled dog a loving home.

Event: Tricks her doppelgänger into breaking into Dwyane Wade’s house
Explanation: She gets amusement while putting another person at risk of legal trouble and violating someone’s property.

Event: Puts question mark stickers on stop signs
Explanation: Confusing stop signs endangers others’ safety with no real benefit beyond fleeting mischief.

Event: Swipes Chris’ car keys so he can’t take Millicent home to have sex
Explanation: She protects Garry’s comfort and slows a situation perceived as inappropriate with little real cost to herself or them.

Event: Consoles Leslie after her argument with Ben and encourages her to talk to him
Explanation: Her emotional support and advice improve Leslie’s chances of fixing the relationship, strengthening their bond.

Event: Offers to take Chris to the movies with tickets she won after feeling guilty about wishing him misfortune
Explanation: She mitigates earlier harm and improves Chris’s mood, while getting social time with Andy and Chris.

Event: Sets Ann up with Tom because she thinks Tom makes Ann happy and hides it from Leslie
Explanation: Her matchmaking is well intentioned and partially successful, even if it later annoys her.

Event: Attempts to set Chris up with Andy’s college professor
Explanation: She tries to help lonely Chris find companionship and also get him off their backs; outcome is messy but not clearly harmful.

Event: Tries hard to be nice to Garry when she believes he is very ill (even scratching his back)
Explanation: She overcomes her usual cruelty to comfort a supposedly sick coworker, gaining empathy practice.

Event: Takes no action when offered to go to Indianapolis with Chris (backs out of possible transfer)
Explanation: Following her gut and staying ultimately leads to a good long-term arc with Andy and her later career.

Event: Chooses to attend time with Andy instead of a gay Halloween party she’d find amazing
Explanation: She sacrifices a fun event for a meaningful night with Andy, strengthening the relationship.

Event: Professionally matches people with projects that fit their passions
Explanation: Her eventual career systematically benefits both individuals and organizations across the country.

Event: Infiltrates a wine tasting and deliberately confuses people with absurd statements
Explanation: She has a great time trolling the event while wasting others’ time and undermining their experience.

Event: Stands up and makes a fake acceptance speech when a sommelier wins and has to be escorted out
Explanation: Her grandstanding entertains herself while hijacking the winner’s moment and disrupting the event.

Event: Violently protects Andy’s feelings and reputation from others’ mistreatment
Explanation: Her fierce defense benefits Andy and usually only mildly inconveniences others.

Event: Lists the things she cares about (Andy, Champion, Leslie, animal shelter) in Season 4
Explanation: Articulating her attachments reflects growth and helps her prioritize caring actions toward them.

Event: Admits, very begrudgingly, that she cares for Ann when saying goodbye
Explanation: This reluctant confession deepens their connection and brings mutual emotional benefit.
